document.title: SCF Level 0 Authority
document.authority_scope: Defines the smallest indivisible architecture invariant set that constrains every lower-level Session Continuity Framework specification and conforming implementation.

field_semantics.interpretation: Descriptive metadata identifies the authority and records provenance without independently imposing conformance requirements. Normative authority defines the Level 0 invariants and hierarchy constraints that all lower levels and implementations must obey.

provenance.preservation: The source document remains preserved as historical bootstrap foundational authority and is not the permanent Level 0 artifact.

architecture_invariants.statement: A Session Continuity Framework application separates the ruleset, dataset, and chatbot responsibilities.
The dataset is the sole authority for committed application information, while the ruleset governs behavior and data-model meaning.
The chatbot and all model or conversation state are transient and must never substitute for committed authority.
Authoritative mutation requires an explicit structured payload, required review or approval, and a controlled commit mechanism that does not invent semantics.
Model context is bounded to the active task and traceable to the higher-level authority that constrains it.
Lower-level specifications and implementations may refine Level 0 authority but must not contradict, override, silently replace, or rename it.
Missing required authority makes work underspecified and is not permission to infer architecture or committed state.

specification_hierarchy.lower_level_parent_rule: Every lower normative specification has exactly one declared normative parent in the accepted authority hierarchy.
specification_hierarchy.refinement_rule: Level 1 and Level 2 specifications may add detail only within the constraints established by higher authority.
specification_hierarchy.implementation_rule: Implementation is non-normative unless authority is explicitly delegated and must conform to all applicable accepted specifications.
specification_hierarchy.scope_boundary: Level 0 defines architecture invariants only; it does not define product-specific runtime architecture, component interfaces, schemas, or application data.
